title: Public Constants and Fields Should Be Static Final
description: Public constants and fields initialized at declaration should be declared as 'static final' rather than just 'final'. This avoids unnecessary duplication of values for every instance of the class, leading to efficient memory usage. Non-static final fields imply that different instances can have different values, which is not the case if they are initialized in the declaration. Therefore, such fields should either be made static or initialized in the constructor.
recommendation: Declare public constants and fields as 'static final' rather than just 'final'. If a non-public, final field isn't also static, ensure it is either made static or initialized in the constructor.
impact: Improper declaration of public constants and fields can lead to unnecessary memory usage and confusion around the values of fields amongst different instances of the class, potentially leading to bugs.

checks.message: Public constants and fields should be declared as 'static final'
